title: The Reigate Squire
synopsis: While recovering from exhaustion at the Surrey home of Watson's friend Colonel Hayter, Holmes is drawn into a local mystery involving a burglary at the house of squire Acton and the subsequent murder of coachman William Kirwan at the Cunningham estate. The torn fragment of a note found clutched in the dead man's hand becomes the key to unravelling the crime.
solution: Old Cunningham and his son Alec are responsible; Kirwan had witnessed the Cunninghams committing the burglary at Acton's house and was blackmailing them, so they murdered him under cover of the widespread burglary scare; Holmes exposes them by analysing the handwriting of the note and provoking them into an attack on him.
notable_elements: Holmes solves the case while supposedly recuperating from nervous exhaustion
Innovative use of handwriting analysis to match the torn note to the Cunninghams
Holmes feigns a physical fit as a ruse to buy time and redirect suspicion
The Cunninghams nearly murder Holmes when he retrieves the incriminating note
